name: Medina Alpine Marathon BTT
name_original: Peña Colorada — circular desde Medina de Pomar
description: Peña Colorada — circular from Medina de Pomar is a difficult 38.6-mile mountain bike route with 3894 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Medina De Pomar offers diverse landscapes, characterized by uneven, rolling terrain and hills, contrasting with the Sierra de Tesla mountain range. The region is crisscrossed by the Nela, Trueba, and Salón rivers, contributing to a lush natural environment and scenic routes through areas like the Losa pine forests. An extensive network of trails, including historical paths and old railway lines, provides varied options for mountain bikers. These routes often pass through significant natural features such as the Ebro River Canyon and the Horadada gorge, offering views of multiple river valleys. Best mountain bike trails around Medina De Pomar The most popular mountain bike trail is Medina Alpine Marathon BTT, a 38.6 miles (62.1 km) difficult trail that takes 5 hours 44 minutes to complete. This demanding route requires good physical condition and biking experience. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Alcázar of the Constables – Santa Clara Convent Museum loop from Medina de Pomar, a difficult 29.7 miles (47.8 km) path. This route combines natural scenery with historical landmarks. Local mountain bikers also love the Puentedey – Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey loop from Medina de Pomar, a 28.1 miles (45.3 km) trail leading through river valleys and geological formations, often completed in about 3 hours 30 minutes. Mountain biking around Medina De Pomar is defined by uneven, rolling terrain, river valleys, and pine forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy singletracks to challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Medina De Pomar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Medina De Pomar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Medina De Pomar?
A: There are over 80 mountain bike trails to explore around Medina De Pomar. These routes cater to various skill levels, with options ranging from easy to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Medina De Pomar?
A: The terrain around Medina De Pomar is diverse, featuring uneven, rolling hills, and contrasting with the Sierra de Tesla mountain range. You'll encounter routes crisscrossed by the Nela, Trueba, and Salón rivers, often passing through lush natural environments and Losa pine forests. Some trails include technical sections with inclines and rocky areas, while others follow historical paths and old railway lines.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Medina De Pomar offers 12 easy mountain bike trails that are suitable for families and beginners. These routes provide a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160185" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Clara Convent Museum – Alcázar of the Constables loop from Medina de Pomar&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 17.3 km route with minimal elevation gain.

Q: Can I use an e-bike on the trails in Medina De Pomar?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Medina De Pomar are suitable for e-bikes, expanding accessibility for riders of all fitness levels. This allows more people to enjoy the diverse landscapes and challenging sections the region offers.

Q: What are some scenic mountain bike routes that offer great views?
A: Many routes in the area are highly scenic. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31467660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puentedey – Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey loop from Medina de Pomar&lt;/a&gt; leads through river valleys and past unique geological formations like the natural tunnel carved by the Nela River. The Camino Natural Santander-Mediterráneo also offers panoramic views of the Trueba, Nela, Ebro, and Oca rivers.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass by waterfalls or other natural landmarks?
A: Yes, the region boasts several natural landmarks. You can find routes that pass near the impressive natural tunnel at Puentedey. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1871481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3970163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tartalés de los Montes Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; are notable natural features that can be incorporated into your rides or visited nearby.

Q: What historical or cultural attractions can I explore along the mountain bike trails?
A: Medina de Pomar offers a blend of natural beauty and historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160472" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcázar of the Constables – Santa Clara Convent Museum loop from Medina de Pomar&lt;/a&gt; combines natural scenery with visits to historical landmarks like the Alcázar de los Velasco (a 14th-century castle-palace) and the Monastery of Santa Clara. The medieval town of Poza de la Sal, with its salt flats, is also a historical-artistic site worth exploring.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Medina De Pomar?
A: The mountain bike trails around Medina De Pomar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and the mix of challenging and accessible routes.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 19 difficult mountain bike trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103235200/medina-alpine-marathon-btt" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medina Alpine Marathon BTT&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 62.1 km route with over 1100 meters of elevation gain, requiring good physical condition and biking experience. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160472" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcázar of the Constables – Santa Clara Convent Museum loop from Medina de Pomar&lt;/a&gt;, a 47.8 km difficult path.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Medina De Pomar are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160472" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcázar of the Constables – Santa Clara Convent Museum loop from Medina de Pomar&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31467660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puentedey – Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey loop from Medina de Pomar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Medina De Pomar?
A: Medina De Pomar is suitable for outdoor activities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, with lush landscapes and comfortable riding conditions. Summer can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are often ideal. Winter riding is possible, though some higher or more exposed trails might be affected by weather conditions.

Q: Are there places to eat or relax near the mountain bike trails?
A: Medina de Pomar itself offers various amenities, including cafes and restaurants. Additionally, the town features several parks and green spaces along the Nela and Trueba rivers, such as Parque de Villacobos and Parque de la Chopera, which are ideal for relaxation and picnics after a ride.

Nice path up to Peña Colorada, a rocky hill with a very unique shape, in the Jerea river plain.

In a marvelous setting, on a well-prominent rocky outcrop, is the Hermitage of San Pantaleon de Losa, which dominates the town's hamlet. The whole set reminds us of the prow of a ship, perfectly visible in its surroundings.

faqs.question: Where does the Peña Colorada — circular from Medina de Pomar trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: While specific trailhead details for this particular route are not readily available in our current data, circular routes typically start and end in the same location. For a trail originating from Medina de Pomar, it's common to find parking options within or on the outskirts of the town. We recommend checking local maps or community forums closer to your visit for precise parking information.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Based on our data, the Peña Colorada — circular from Medina de Pomar route is rated as difficult. This suggests it's best suited for experienced mountain bikers with good fitness and technical skills. Beginners might find the terrain and elevation challenging.

faqs.question: How long should I expect to spend mountain biking this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 62 km long with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain. Our estimated duration for completing it is around 5 hours and 44 minutes. However, your actual time will vary depending on your fitness level, the number of stops you make, and trail conditions.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: This route features several notable highlights. You'll pass by the historic Alcázar of the Constables, experience the challenging Ascent to Peña Colorada, and discover the serene Hermitage of San Pantaleón de Losa.

faqs.question: Is the Peña Colorada — circular from Medina de Pomar trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, mountain biking trails in natural areas are dog-friendly, especially if they are not within protected national parks with strict regulations. However, specific rules regarding dogs (e.g., leash requirements) for this particular route are not available. It's always best to keep your dog under control and be mindful of other trail users and potential wildlife.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Given its location, spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant weather for mountain biking in this region, with milder temperatures and less chance of extreme heat or cold. Summer can be hot, and winter might bring colder temperatures or snow at higher elevations. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: There is no indication in our current information that a permit or entrance fee is required to access the Peña Colorada — circular from Medina de Pomar route. Most trails in Spain are freely accessible. However, it's always a good idea to verify locally if you have any concerns.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Maratón Alpina Medinesa 42 KM, Caminos de Medina, and the Asturian Way, among others. This can offer opportunities to explore different parts of the region if you wish to extend or modify your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: For a difficult, nearly 6-hour mountain bike ride, you should bring essential gear. This includes a well-maintained mountain bike, helmet, appropriate cycling attire, plenty of water and snacks, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), a first-aid kit, and navigation tools (like a GPS device or the komoot app with the downloaded tour).

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to resupply near the route?
faqs.answer: Starting and ending in Medina de Pomar, you'll find various amenities, including cafes, restaurants, and shops, within the town itself. However, along the 62 km route, specific resupply points are not detailed in our current data. It's advisable to carry enough provisions for the entire duration of your ride.
